title: TRANQUIL – Privacy Dashboard, Data Ownership Controls & Secure Deletion

requirements.summary: Implement Privacy Dashboard page with data summary, privacy toggles, device management, and account deletion button
acceptanceCriteria: A /privacy route is accessible from Settings and Profile pages.
Data summary cards show real counts fetched from the backend for stress logs, mood entries, sleep entries, breathing sessions, and paired devices.
Analytics tracking toggle and AI prediction toggle are rendered as Switch components and their state is persisted to the backend user profile.
Connected devices section lists stored pairing records; each entry has a 'Remove Device' button that deletes that record from the backend.
Delete Account button is styled with a red background and the label 'Delete My Account'.
Page is fully responsive at 320 px, 768 px, and 1024 px+ viewports.
Dark and light themes render correctly with no visual discrepancies.

file_operations.description: Create the Privacy Dashboard page component that fetches user data counts, displays data summary cards (stress logs, mood entries, sleep entries, breathing sessions, device pairings), renders analytics and AI prediction toggles using the Switch component, displays a Manage Connected Devices section with individual device removal buttons, and includes a prominently styled red 'Delete My Account' button at the bottom. Use the useGetUserPrivacyPreferences query hook to fetch privacy settings and device pairings. Use the useUpdatePrivacyPreferences mutation for toggle changes and useRemoveDevicePairing mutation for device removal. Apply TRANQUIL design system styling (OKLCH palette, Nunito/Quicksand fonts, dark/light theme support). Ensure full responsiveness from 320px mobile through desktop breakpoints. Include a plain-language 'Data Usage Explanation' section describing what data is collected, why it is used, and retention periods.

Register the /privacy route in the TanStack Router configuration, mapping it to the Privacy page component. Ensure the route is protected by the existing AuthGuard so only authenticated users can access it.
Add a navigation link/button to the Privacy Dashboard (/privacy) in the Settings page. Place it prominently in the main settings menu, labeled 'Privacy & Data Control' or similar.
Add a navigation link/button to the Privacy Dashboard (/privacy) in the Profile page. Place it near the existing action buttons (e.g., near logout), labeled 'Privacy Dashboard' or 'Manage My Data'.

requirements.summary: Implement full account deletion flow with confirmation modal, backend soft-delete call, and logout
acceptanceCriteria: Clicking 'Delete My Account' opens a confirmation modal with the exact warning text.
The confirm button remains disabled until the user types 'DELETE' in the input field.
On confirmation, the backend `requestAccountDeletion` function is called and returns success.
The backend marks the user record with `pendingDeletion = true` and records `deletionScheduledAt` timestamp.
After confirmation, all React Query caches are cleared and the user is logged out via Internet Identity.
A success toast 'Your account deletion has been scheduled. You have 30 days to cancel.' is shown before logout.
The entire flow from button click to logout completes within 30 seconds.

file_operations.description: Create a confirmation modal component for account deletion. Display the exact warning text 'This action is permanent and cannot be undone.' and a secondary message explaining that all data (profile, stress logs, mood journal, sleep data, device pairing history) will be deleted. Include a text input field requiring the user to type 'DELETE' to enable the confirm button. Use the Dialog component from shadcn/ui. On confirmation, call the useRequestAccountDeletion mutation hook, clear all React Query caches using queryClient.clear(), show a success toast with the message 'Your account deletion has been scheduled. You have 30 days to cancel.', and trigger logout via the useInternetIdentity hook's clear() method. Style the confirm button with red/destructive styling. Ensure the entire flow is non-blocking and completes quickly.
Wire the Delete Account button to open the DeleteAccountModal component when clicked. Import and render the DeleteAccountModal, managing its open/close state with local React state.

requirements.summary: Implement selective data deletion controls for individual entries and bulk data clearing
acceptanceCriteria: Each stress log entry in the Analytics page and any log list renders a delete icon button.
Clicking a stress log delete icon shows an inline 'Are you sure?' prompt; confirming calls the backend and removes the entry from the UI.
Each mood journal entry in the Journal page renders a delete icon button with the same inline confirmation pattern.
Privacy Dashboard 'Clear Analytics History' button triggers a backend call that removes all stress readings for the user; UI reflects the cleared state.
Privacy Dashboard 'Reset Device Data' button triggers a backend call that removes all device pairing records; the Manage Connected Devices list becomes empty.
All four backend functions (`deleteStressReading`, `deleteMoodEntry`, `clearAnalyticsHistory`, `clearDevicePairings`) exist in `backend/main.mo` with proper authorization checks.

file_operations.description: Add a delete icon button (Trash2 from Lucide) to each stress log entry in the daily breakdown list. When clicked, display an inline confirmation prompt ('Are you sure?') using an AlertDialog or simple confirm pattern. On confirmation, call the useDeleteStressReading mutation with the reading's ID, invalidate the stress readings query cache, and remove the entry from the UI optimistically. Handle error cases with a toast notification.

Add a delete icon button (Trash2 from Lucide) to each mood journal entry in the past entries list. When clicked, display an inline confirmation prompt ('Are you sure?'). On confirmation, call the useDeleteMoodEntry mutation with the entry's ID, invalidate the mood entries query cache, and remove the entry from the UI optimistically. Handle error cases with a toast notification.

Add a 'Manage My Data' section to the Privacy Dashboard with two destructive action buttons: 'Clear Analytics History' and 'Reset Device Data'. Each button should display an inline confirmation dialog before executing. 'Clear Analytics History' calls the useClearAnalyticsHistory mutation and invalidates the stress readings cache. 'Reset Device Data' calls the useClearDevicePairings mutation and invalidates the device pairings cache. Both buttons should be styled with destructive/warning colors and show success toasts on completion.

requirements.summary: Enhance data export feature with JSON, CSV, and PDF format options
acceptanceCriteria: Settings page export section shows a format selector with JSON, CSV, and PDF options.
Selecting JSON and clicking download triggers the existing JSON export, producing a timestamped `.json` file.
Selecting CSV and clicking download produces a `.csv` file with columns: date, stressScore, heartRate, skinTemp, motion.

Selecting PDF and clicking download produces a `.pdf` file (or a `.html` print-ready file) with a structured summary report including user name, total sessions, average stress score, and a readable 7-day data table.
`frontend/src/utils/dataExport.ts` exports `exportAsCSV` and `exportAsPDF` functions.
All downloads use a timestamped filename pattern (e.g., `tranquil-export-2024-01-15.csv`).

file_operations.description: Add two new export utility functions: `exportAsCSV(data: UserDataView, filename: string)` that converts stress readings into a flat CSV with columns (date, stressScore, heartRate, skinTemp, motion) and triggers a browser download; and `exportAsPDF(data: UserDataView, profile: UserProfile, filename: string)` that generates a structured HTML-based summary report (or uses a library like jsPDF) including user name, avatar, total sessions, average stress score, mood trend, and a 7-day stress data table, then triggers download as a PDF or print-ready HTML file. Both functions should use timestamped filenames following the pattern `tranquil-export-YYYY-MM-DD.<ext>`.

Add a format selector (RadioGroup or SegmentedControl with options: JSON, CSV, PDF) above the existing data export download button. Update the download button's click handler to call the appropriate export function based on the selected format: `exportUserData` for JSON, `exportAsCSV` for CSV, or `exportAsPDF` for PDF. Ensure the user data and profile are fetched via the existing useGetUserData query before triggering the export.

requirements.summary: Add GDPR/compliance UI layer with Terms & Conditions acceptance modal and Privacy Policy links
acceptanceCriteria: First-time users see a T&C / Privacy Policy acceptance modal before the profile setup modal.
The 'Continue' button on the acceptance modal is disabled until the acceptance checkbox is checked.
Accepting the T&C calls a backend function that records `termsAcceptedAt` timestamp on the user profile.
Users who have already accepted are not shown the modal again on subsequent logins.
Settings page footer contains 'Privacy Policy' and 'Terms & Conditions' links that open a read-only modal.
Privacy Dashboard includes a 'Data Usage Explanation' section with plain-language descriptions of data collection, purpose, and retention period.
`UserProfile` in `backend/main.mo` includes a `termsAcceptedAt: ?Int` field and a `recordTermsAcceptance()` function.

file_operations.description: Create a modal component for first-time Terms & Conditions and Privacy Policy acceptance. Display a scrollable summary of data practices, a checkbox labeled 'I accept the Terms & Conditions and Privacy Policy', and a 'Continue' button that remains disabled until the checkbox is checked. On Continue, call the useRecordTermsAcceptance mutation hook to record the timestamp in the backend user profile, then close the modal. Use the Dialog component from shadcn/ui. Style consistently with TRANQUIL design system.

Create a read-only modal component for displaying full Privacy Policy or Terms & Conditions text. Accept a `type` prop ('privacy' | 'terms') to determine which policy to display. Use the Dialog component from shadcn/ui with scrollable content and a close button. Style consistently with TRANQUIL design system.

Update the AuthGuard to check whether the user has accepted terms using the useGetUserPrivacyPreferences query (checking for termsAcceptedAt field). If authenticated but termsAcceptedAt is null, render the TermsAcceptanceModal before showing the ProfileSetupModal. Ensure proper sequencing: login → terms acceptance → profile setup → app access.

Add a footer section to the Settings page with links for 'Privacy Policy' and 'Terms & Conditions'. Each link should open the PolicyModal component with the appropriate type prop. Style the links as subtle text links with hover states.

Ensure the Privacy Dashboard includes a 'Data Usage Explanation' section (if not already added in REQ-3) with plain-language descriptions of what data is collected (stress readings, mood entries, sleep data, breathing sessions, device pairings), why it is used (personalized wellness insights, trend analysis), and how long it is retained (indefinitely until user deletion or account removal). Style as an informational card or section with readable typography.

requirements.summary: Update React Query hooks to include all new privacy and deletion mutations and queries
acceptanceCriteria: All listed mutation hooks exist and are exported from `useQueries.ts`.
Each mutation hook calls the corresponding backend actor method.
On success, each mutation invalidates the appropriate React Query cache keys (e.g., `['userProfile']`, `['stressReadings']`, `['moodEntries']`).
`useGetUserPrivacyPreferences` returns `analyticsEnabled`, `aiPredictionEnabled`, `devicePairings`, `pendingDeletion`, and `termsAcceptedAt` from the user profile query.
TypeScript types are correctly inferred with no `any` casts.

file_operations.description: Add the following new mutation hooks: `useRequestAccountDeletion`, `useCancelAccountDeletion`, `useDeleteStressReading`, `useDeleteMoodEntry`, `useClearAnalyticsHistory`, `useAddDevicePairing`, `useRemoveDevicePairing`, `useClearDevicePairings`, `useUpdatePrivacyPreferences`, `useRecordTermsAcceptance`. Each mutation should call the corresponding backend actor method and invalidate relevant query cache keys on success (['userProfile'], ['stressReadings'], ['moodEntries'], ['userData'], etc.). Add a new query hook `useGetUserPrivacyPreferences` that reads `analyticsEnabled`, `aiPredictionEnabled`, `devicePairings`, `pendingDeletion`, and `termsAcceptedAt` from the user profile query (this can be derived from the existing `useGetCallerUserProfile` query or be a separate focused query). Ensure proper TypeScript typing throughout with no `any` casts.

requirements.summary: Apply cohesive visual upgrade with gradients, glassmorphism, glow effects, and animations
acceptanceCriteria: Dashboard hero section displays a teal-to-indigo gradient background.
Card components use a frosted-glass / glassmorphism surface style with backdrop-blur.
Stress gauge and breathing circle have a soft ambient glow effect.
Page transitions include a fade-in + slide-up entrance animation.
All interactive elements (buttons, tiles, toggles) have visible hover and active press states.
New accent color tokens (teal, sage, gold) are defined as OKLCH CSS variables in `frontend/src/index.css`.
Visual consistency is maintained across light mode, dark mode, mobile, tablet, and desktop viewports.

file_operations.description: Add new OKLCH CSS custom property tokens for jewel-tone accent colors (teal, sage green, soft gold) in both light and dark theme blocks. Define gradient variables for teal-to-indigo backgrounds. Add utility classes or update existing tokens for glassmorphism card surfaces (backdrop-blur, semi-transparent backgrounds). Ensure all new color tokens follow the existing OKLCH variable structure and are theme-aware (light/dark variants).

Extend the Tailwind theme configuration to include the new jewel-tone accent colors (teal, sage, gold) as custom color utilities. Add animation keyframes for fade-in and slide-up entrance animations. Define backdrop-blur utility classes if not already present. Ensure all extensions are compatible with existing TRANQUIL design system tokens.

Update the Dashboard hero section to use a teal-to-indigo gradient background (using the new CSS variables or Tailwind gradient utilities). Apply the gradient as a background overlay or container background. Ensure the greeting and stress gauge remain visually prominent against the gradient. Verify responsiveness and theme consistency.

Add a soft ambient glow effect to the stress gauge SVG using CSS filters (drop-shadow or box-shadow with blur and color matching the stress level). Apply glow dynamically based on the current stress level (low/medium/high). Ensure the glow is subtle and enhances visual appeal without overwhelming the gauge display.

Add a soft ambient glow effect to the breathing circle element using CSS filters or radial gradient overlays. Apply the glow during the inhale/exhale phases to enhance the calming visual. Ensure the animation remains smooth and the glow effect is theme-aware.

Add global CSS classes or animations for page entrance effects (fade-in + slide-up) on route transitions. Apply these animations to the main content Outlet wrapper or individual page containers. Use Tailwind's animate utilities or CSS @keyframes. Ensure animations are subtle, fast (200-300ms), and do not interfere with navigation or accessibility.

Enhance all button variants with visible hover and active press states using scale transforms, brightness adjustments, or shadow changes. Ensure transitions are smooth (150-200ms) and apply consistently across all button sizes and variants. Verify accessibility (focus states remain visible).

Update Card component styles to use glassmorphism surface styling: semi-transparent backgrounds using the new CSS variables, backdrop-blur utility classes, and subtle borders. Ensure the frosted-glass effect works in both light and dark themes and remains readable over gradient backgrounds.
